page.title: Channels
page.subtitle: Connect your OpenClaw agents to external messaging platforms and APIs.

feedback.loadFailed: Unable to load channel configuration right now.
feedback.loadFailedServiceUnavailable: Channel service is temporarily unavailable. Confirm the instance and kernel are running, then retry.
feedback.saveFailed: Unable to save channel configuration right now.
feedback.deleteFailed: Unable to delete channel configuration right now.
feedback.toggleFailed: Unable to update channel status right now.

panel.qrConnectionDescription: Open the official OpenClaw binding flow, then scan the platform QR code shown by the runtime or connector.
panel.qrConnectionHint: Run the commands in a trusted terminal, scan the QR prompt in the official app, then restart the OpenClaw gateway.

descriptions.media: Broadcast and public account delivery surfaces
descriptions.all: All channels available in this workspace

empty.domestic: No domestic chat channels are available in this workspace.
empty.global: No global chat channels are available in this workspace.
empty.media: No media account channels are available in this workspace.
empty.all: No chat channels are available in this workspace.

steps.add: Start the official OpenClaw QQ Bot add wizard and choose the QR-code beginner guide.
steps.scan: Scan the QR code with QQ, then finish the platform authorization requested by the wizard.
steps.restart: Restart the OpenClaw gateway after the QQ Bot wizard reports that the channel is ready.

steps.install: Install the official Tencent WeChat OpenClaw plugin and follow the terminal QR prompt.
steps.scan: Scan the QR code with WeChat and approve the binding in the mobile client.
steps.restart: Restart the OpenClaw gateway so the newly bound channel is loaded.

steps.login: Start the OpenClaw Feishu login flow from the runtime CLI or documented setup wizard.
steps.scan: Scan the platform QR code with Feishu or complete the app authorization requested by the runtime.
steps.restart: Restart or reload OpenClaw after the runtime reports that Feishu binding is ready.

steps.install: Install the official DingTalk OpenClaw connector from DingTalk Real AI.
steps.scan: Scan the QR code displayed by the connector installer with DingTalk and finish authorization.
steps.restart: Restart the OpenClaw gateway so the connector can publish the bound channel.

contextVisibility.label: Context Visibility
contextVisibility.helpText: Optional per-channel context visibility policy, for example quote, none, or allowlist_quote.

qqbot.name: QQ Bot
qqbot.description: Connect OpenClaw to QQ direct messages and groups through the official QQ Bot API.

setupSteps.0: Open the OpenClaw QQ Bot channel documentation before entering credentials.
setupSteps.1: Configure the QQ Bot Official API plugin with the runtime-supported fields shown here.
setupSteps.2: Enable QQ Bot after credentials or runtime account state are ready.

appId.label: App ID
appId.helpText: Required QQ Bot application ID from the QQ Bot developer console.

clientSecret.label: Client Secret
clientSecret.helpText: Required QQ Bot client secret. Prefer an environment or secret reference for release deployments.

clientSecretFile.label: Client Secret File
clientSecretFile.helpText: Optional file or secret reference used instead of an inline client secret.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional direct-message allowlist of QQ user IDs. Enter one sender per line or a JSON array.

groupAllowFrom.label: Group Allow From
groupAllowFrom.helpText: Optional QQ group allowlist used when groupPolicy is allowlist. Enter one group ID per line or a JSON array.

dmPolicy.label: DM Policy
dmPolicy.helpText: Optional direct-message policy: open, allowlist, or disabled.

groupPolicy.label: Group Policy
groupPolicy.helpText: Optional group policy: open, allowlist, or disabled.

systemPrompt.label: System Prompt
systemPrompt.placeholder: Optional QQ Bot specific system prompt

markdownSupport.label: Markdown Support
markdownSupport.helpText: Optional boolean flag controlling QQ Markdown output support.

streaming.label: Streaming
streaming.helpText: Optional QQ Bot streaming configuration. Use true, false, or a JSON object supported by the plugin.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of QQ group routing rules. Use group IDs or "*" to define defaults.

stt.label: Speech To Text
stt.helpText: Optional QQ Bot speech-to-text configuration. Use true, false, or a plugin-supported JSON object.

tts.label: Text To Speech
tts.helpText: Optional QQ Bot text-to-speech configuration. Use true, false, or a plugin-supported JSON object.

audioFormatPolicy.label: Audio Format Policy
audioFormatPolicy.helpText: Optional QQ Bot audio format policy for direct STT/upload formats and plugin transcoding.

accounts.label: Accounts
accounts.helpText: Optional multi-account QQ Bot configuration keyed by account ID.

imessage.name: iMessage
imessage.description: Bridge OpenClaw into iMessage or SMS conversations through the bundled iMessage plugin.

setupSteps.0: Open the OpenClaw iMessage channel documentation before entering credentials.
setupSteps.1: Configure the iMessage plugin with the runtime-supported fields shown here.
setupSteps.2: Enable iMessage after credentials or runtime account state are ready.

dbPath.label: Database Path
dbPath.helpText: Optional iMessage database path when the runtime cannot use the platform default.

remoteHost.label: Remote Host
remoteHost.helpText: Optional remote iMessage bridge host for non-local runtime access.

service.label: Service
service.helpText: Optional service selector: imessage, sms, or auto.

region.label: SMS Region
region.helpText: Optional SMS region used by the iMessage plugin.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional direct-message allowlist. Enter one sender per line or a JSON array. Use "*" only for intentionally open trusted channels.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of group or room routing rules. Use "*" to define defaults for all groups.

irc.description: Bridge OpenClaw into classic IRC networks with server, nick, and channel routing controls.

setupSteps.0: Open the OpenClaw IRC channel documentation before entering credentials.
setupSteps.1: Configure the IRC plugin with the runtime-supported fields shown here.
setupSteps.2: Enable IRC after credentials or runtime account state are ready.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional direct-message allowlist. Enter one sender per line or a JSON array. Use "*" only for intentionally open trusted channels.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of group or room routing rules. Use "*" to define defaults for all groups.

account.label: Account

allowFrom.helpText: Optional direct-message allowlist. Enter one sender per line or a JSON array. Use "*" only for intentionally open trusted channels.

telegram.name: Telegram
telegram.description: Connect OpenClaw to Telegram direct messages and groups through the Bot API.

setupSteps.0: Open the OpenClaw Telegram channel documentation before entering credentials.
setupSteps.1: Configure the Telegram Bot plugin with the runtime-supported fields shown here.
setupSteps.2: Enable Telegram after credentials or runtime account state are ready.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional allowlist of Telegram sender IDs or usernames. Enter one sender per line or a JSON array.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of Telegram group rules. Use chat IDs or "*" to define defaults.

errorPolicy.label: Error Policy
errorPolicy.helpText: Optional Telegram delivery error policy, for example retry or disable.
